provenance.source_corpus: Restoring a wetland complex for amphibian populations, south Okanagan Valley, British Columbia, Canada (2003 to 2014)

source_spans.text: wetland construction and enhancement can mitigate anthropogenic stressors in degraded landscapes for pond species.

Highway expansion in the south Okanagan continues to bisect breeding sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:ec42b661fa20
edges.original_sentence: Our 'small pond' project supports our hypothesis that strategically constructed and enhanced ponds can provide breeding habitat aiding in the Species at Risk (SAR) recovery of Great Basin spadefoots, in the lower south Okanagan Valley.

source_spans.text: Our 'small pond' project supports our hypothesis that strategically constructed and enhanced ponds can provide breeding habitat aiding in the Species at Risk (SAR) recovery of Great Basin spadefoots, in the lower south Okanagan Valley.
